properties.name: Jorbeed Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: Rajasthan's first Conservation Reserve — notified 2015 to protect the migratory raptor congregation at Bikaner's Jorbeed carcass dump.
Home to Rajasthan's densest wintering raptor population — Steppe Eagle, Egyptian Vulture, Griffon, Cinereous Vulture congregate here (~5,000 birds).
Global significance — one of only 3 sites in India where the Endangered Egyptian Vulture reliably winters in large numbers.

properties.name: Sundhamata Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: Aravalli hill forest around the Sundha Mata shrine — Rajasthan's third-highest peak (Sundha Mata Peak, 1,220 m).
Straddles Jalore-Sirohi boundary; protects the corridor between Mount Abu WLS and Kumbhalgarh WLS.
Sloth bear, leopard, chinkara, langur; Aravalli's most southerly leopard population.

properties.name: Gudha Bishnoiyan Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: Protects the community-conserved khejri-and-blackbuck landscape of the Bishnoi villages south of Jodhpur.
The 1730 Khejarli martyrdom (363 Bishnois died protecting khejri trees) happened in this cluster — the world's first recorded environmental sacrifice.
One of India's few CRs where local community management is written into the notification itself.

properties.name: Beed Ghas Farm Conservation Reserve, Jhunjhunu
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: One of Rajasthan's earliest CRs — a semi-arid grass-and-scrub reserve carved out of the erstwhile Beed Ghas Farm.
Shelter for chinkara, blackbuck, desert fox, migratory bustards in the Shekhawati belt.
Community-managed alongside the Rajasthan Forest Department — one of the state's first co-managed reserves.

properties.name: Rotu Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: One of India's smallest Conservation Reserves — just 0.72 sq km — but a critical demoiselle-crane wintering site in the Nagaur belt.
The community-conserved pond at Rotu village hosts ~4,000 demoiselle cranes (Kurja) from November to March.
Second only to Khichan (Phalodi) in Rajasthan for demoiselle-crane winter concentration.

properties.name: Basi Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: Dry-deciduous forest around Basi village near Chittorgarh; contiguous with the Bassi Wildlife Sanctuary.
Supports leopard, chausingha (four-horned antelope), sloth bear and migratory water-birds at Basi Talab.
Together with Bassi WLS forms one of eastern Rajasthan's largest continuous forest corridors.

properties.name: Bansiya-Bhagi Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: Extends the Ramgarh Vishdhari Tiger Reserve corridor into the Chambal ravines of Bundi-Kota.
Buffers the Mukundra Hills TR and the Ranthambhore TR — key to the tiger movement corridor across southeast Rajasthan.
Rich in leopard, hyena, chinkara, and Chambal ravine bird species (Bonelli's Eagle, Chestnut-shouldered Petronia).

properties.name: Shakambhari Conservation Reserve
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Rajasthan Forest Dept notifications

facts: Aravalli forest around the Shakambhari Devi shrine — the historical hearth of the Chauhans of Shakambhari (7th–12th c.).
Corridor between the Sariska TR and the Jaisamand-Bassi complex; migratory route for leopards and sambar.
The Shakambhari temple is Rajasthan's oldest continuously worshipped Shakti shrine — dates to the Gupta era.
